_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The Big 12 is a P4 conference but trails the Big Ten and SEC in talent and funding — its CFP candidates almost always come through as the conference's auto-bid champion, not as at-large dark horses. Three storylines define the 2026 Big 12: (1) Texas Tech is the preseason favorite, but national title aspirations were tied to QB Brendan Sorsby, a portal addition from Cincinnati who's likely to miss the season via NCAA suspension due to gambling accusations. Texas Tech won the 2025 conference title and made the CFP (lost to Oregon, offense limited). (2) Oklahoma State bounce-back — new HC Eric Morris brought a critical mass of his North Texas players with him via the portal; expect a meaningfully different team than the dismal 2025 version. (3) Two of the league's top players will likely miss the season for off-field reasons — Sorsby (above) and BYU WR Parker Kingston, who is facing serious criminal charges; the agent should write the Kingston situation with formal/legal language and NOT speculate beyond what has been publicly reported. Volatility calibration: the Big 12 has the loosest hierarchy in the P4 — 16 teams playing a 9-game conference schedule means nobody plays a clean cross-section, and recent results show multiple different champions and a preseason favorite that misses the title game more often than not.  When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. ACC tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = ACC title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound (typically as the conference auto-bid, not an at-large); Tier 2 = title-game contender, dark-horse CFP at-large; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = bottom of the conference, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. Big 12 Championship game is played in Arlington, Texas.

team_notes.arizona: Tier 2-4 Big 12 team. HC Brent Brennan jumped from 4-8 in 2024 to 9-4 in 2025, has a good staff in OC Seth Doege and DC Danny Gonzales.  QB Noah Fifita is the best in the Big 12, but WR is rebuilding.  RBs & WRs have some experienced options but remains to be seen who takes the next step.  OL should be decent with two returning starters and two portal starters added.   Defense is replacing the best four players from last season, DL and secondary especially need rebulding.  LBs are stength with Taye Brown returning and Everett Roussaw Jr. added via the portal from Memphis.  Secondary will be manned by mostly portals who have experienced players but mostly from G6 & FCS.  On paper this is a mid level Big 12 team.
team_notes.byu: Tier 2 Big 12 team. Big 12 runner up in 2025, lost to Texas Tech in the Big 12 championship as well as in the regular season, undefeated vs everyone else.  HC Kalani Sitake is a good one that turned down an offer from Penn State to be their HC. Offensive Coordinator Aaron Roderick is a good one, but DC Kelly Poppinga, last years special teams coach, is new DC replacing departed DC Jay Hill who left for Michigan DC job. QB Bear Bachmeier played great as a freshman last year, RB LJ Martin is the best RB in the Big 12.  Wide receiver is a huge question after Parker Kingston was kicked off the team.  WR portal addition Kyler Kasper from Oregon and Tight end Walker Lyons are expected to step up this fall.  Defense expects to be good again this fall, CB Evan Johnson and safety Faletau Satuala are potential All Big 12 candidates.
team_notes.cincinnati: Tier 4-5 Big 12 team. Lost QB Brendan Sorsby to Texas Tech via transfer portal.  HC Scott Satterfield is a replacement level coach at best who needs a good 2026 season to keep any hope from fanbase.  Both OC and DC are new, but new DC Nate Woody comes from 6 seasons as the DC at Army where he did a commendable job.  Defensive front seven looks to be the best unit on the team, although the secondary is rebuilt via portals from the G6.  QB JC French IV from Georgia Southern is average at best but brings experience.  Have a couple of decent RBs via the transfer portal, WR top portal additions are from the FCS.  Cincinnati looks like a bottom Big 12 team on paper with a HC that's likely on the clock to be replaced.
team_notes.colorado: Tier 4-5 Big 12 team. HC Deion Sanders has real health issues and his tenure has gone from 9-4 in 2024 to only 3-9 in 2025.  Lost top OT Jordan Seaton to LSU in the transfer portal, the top two WRs from last season also departed via the portal.  QB Julian Lewis played well as a freshman last fall when he got his chance to start, but WR health has been an issue in spring.  Rush offense in Sanders tenure has been terrible, and OL play a constant issue.  Colorado did add a ton of production via the transfer portal, WRs Danny Scudero from San Jose State and Kam Perry from Miami (Ohio) along with six OL players that started last season at previous schools.  Defense is a big question although the secondary was a real bright spot in the spring and looks to be the rosters most improved unit.  Colorado is a lower tier Big 12 team, making a bowl game looks to be the best case scenario.
team_notes.houston: Tier 2-3 Big 12 team.  HC Willie Fritz is in his 3rd year, jumping from 4-8 in 2024 to 10-3 in 2025 and looks to be a potential contender in the Big 12.  QB Conner Weigman returns as a second year starter who's a great mobile threat and a much improved passer.  RB is rebuilding around Makhi Hughes who was great at Tulane in 2024 but was unable to get on the field at Oregon last season.  WR Amare Thomas is a superstar and Trent Walker was added via the portal from Oregon State to form possibly the Big 12's best duo.  OL looks strong with two returning starters and 3 good portal starters added headlined by Shadre Hurst from Tulane.  Amar DC Austin Armstrong is a good one, but must rebuild the defense especially the DL, but DE/LB looks strong with guys like Sione Fotu and Brandon Mack III.
team_notes.iowa-state: Tier 3-4 Big 12 team. Lost all time winningest HC Matt Campbell to Penn State, hired HC Jimmy Rogers from Washington State.  Lost 24 portal players to Penn State as well along with Campbell and OC Taylor Mouser. Roster is a complete rebuild with over 70 new players.  QB Jaylen Raynor is a portal add from Arkansas State and a fun one but has had turnover issues.  RB & WR all portal adds, WR Omari Hayes from Tulane the headliner. Spring standout RB Jayden Jackson left the team in early May, with HC Rogers commenting publicly about attitude and off field standards.  Defense looked much more settled than Offense in spring, but This team could really be a mess this fall, only time will tell.
team_notes.kansas-state: Tier 2-3 Big 12 team. New Collin Klein was former Kansas State QB, was most recently the offensive coordinator at Texas A&M.  New OC Sean Gleeson hasn't called plays since 2022 and new DC Jordan Peterson was with Klien at Texas A&M as the co-DC. QB Avery Johnson returns for his 3rd year as starter, he is a former big recruit and a good mobile threat that hasn't lived up to expectations as a passer. RB Joe Jackson returns and is a good one, WR added three portals to find better weapons to pair with TE Garrett Oakley, a PFF top 10 rated TE.  OL has only one starter returning to pair with 2 portal starters, a genuine question mark. The defense is also a question, although portal additions like DE Wendell Gregory from Oklahoma State and LB Mekhi Mason from Louisiana Tech help.  New HC, questions at OL and defense make Kansas State a mid level Big 12 team on paper.
team_notes.texas-tech: Tier 1 Big 12 team. Won the 2025 Big 12 championship, lost in CFP to Oregon due to a limited offense. QB is likely Will Hammond who is still recovering from a torn ACL last season, status for start of 2026 is questionable but will return by October.  QB portal addition Brendan Sorsby is caught up in NCAA violations due to gambling accusations and will likely be suspended by the NCAA; Sorsby entered gambling rehab in early May.  Roster is loaded with talent, Texas Tech is still the Big 12 clear favorite despite the QB issues above.  RBs Cameron Dickey and J'Koby Williams are the best duo in the Big 12. TE Terrance Carter Jr. may be the best weapon in the Big 12, although WR is rebuilding via the portal but has added good pieces.  Defense was dominant last year and while likely to regress some, it will still be the best defense in the conference, led by a DL returning A.J. Holmes at DT to pair with portal addition Mateen Ibirogba from Wake Forest.  HC Joey McGuire has hired one of CFBs top staff in defensive coordinator Shiel Wood and offensive coordinator Mack Leftwich is also a really good one.  Big 12 favorite, favorite to make the CFP.
team_notes.utah: Tier 2 Big 12 team. New HC Morgan Scalley was Utah defensive coordinator for nine years.  Utah pushed out all time winningest HC Kyle Whittingham so Scalley could take over which adds a layer of intruige.  Colton Swan takes over at DC but will be his first year calling plays, new OC is veteran Kevin McGiven most recently from Utah State.  QB Devon Dampier returns after a good year last year but remains a better threat with his legs than his arm.  RB Wayshawn Parker is a good one returning, but WR is rebuilding from the portal such as Braden Pegan from Utah State.  OL is rebuilding around two part time starters back and portal addition Cedric Jefferson from Middle Tennessee.  Defense returns 70% with a great looking secondary headlined by players like CB Elijah Reed and Safety Jackson Bennee. DL is rebuilt via portals that are mostly from the G6, a genuine question mark.
